memory_recovered: Canonical continuity integration requires a separately retained, commit-bound candidate-generation event. The canonical index, corrected successor delta, generated candidate, source commit, base blob identity, test output, validation output, and semantic review must remain distinguishable evidence objects.

verification_contract.exact_checkout: workflow requires git rev-parse HEAD to equal GITHUB_SHA
verification_contract.exact_base_binding: workflow requires git hash-object continuity-index.json to equal delta.base_index.content_sha
verification_contract.delta_validation: validate-continuity-delta.mjs against the observed base blob
verification_contract.candidate_generation: merge-continuity-delta.mjs writes a noncanonical candidate file
semantic_checks: appended record identifiers exact and ordered
retained_outputs: expected and observed base blob identities

limits: The workflow has been committed and source-inspected but no completed workflow run is claimed in this evidence packet.
The generated candidate is not canonical and the workflow has read-only repository permissions.
A passing workflow would establish deterministic merge readiness, not truth of every indexed claim.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, hashes, schema vocabulary, and abstract continuity mechanics only. Raw private chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads remain excluded.
falsification_or_review_route: Run the workflow at an immutable commit, inspect all retained outputs, and reject merge readiness if checkout identity, base blob identity, tests, validator, candidate semantics, or privacy preservation fail.
